title: carex atlantica subsp. capillacea
intro: Carex atlantica subsp. capillacea, also known as Atlantic sedge, is a subspecies of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae and is native to the Atlantic coast of Europe and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Carex atlantica subsp. capillacea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Carex atlantica subsp. capillacea can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, or used in salves and creams to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
